October 2025 |module = {{infobox person |embed = yes |father = |mother = |spouse = |education = |alma_mater = |relatives = [[Darrell Griffin]] (brother)
[[Josh Griffin]] (brother) }} }} '''George Griffin''' (born {{birth date|1992|6|26|df=y}}) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[London Broncos]], [[Salford Red Devils]], and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time at [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] in the [[2013 Championship 1|Championship 1]] on dual registration from Hull KR, and at [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the Super League on loan from Salford. ==Background== Griffin was born in [[Oxford]], Oxfordshire, England. He is the younger brother to [[Josh Griffin|Josh]] and [[Darrell Griffin]]. Griffin previously played [[rugby union]] while growing up in Oxford, before moving to rugby league alongside his brothers. He played as a junior for [[Stanley Rangers]]. In October 2023, working together with former [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] teammate [[Bureta Faraimo]], Griffin released a children's book titled ''Harvey Hippo Finds His Talent''.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=23 Oct 2023|title=Former Castleford Tigers team-mates link up to release children's book: Harvey Hippo Finds His Talent|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-castleford-tigers-team-mates-link-up-to-release-childrens-book-harvey-hippo-finds-his-talent}} His follow-up book, ''Harvey Hippo Joins The Band'', was commissioned in May 2024 and released in October 2024.{{Cite web |last=Willacy |first=Gavin |date=23 May 2024 |title=Meet George Griffin, the polymath rugby player and children's author |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/article/2024/may/23/george-griffin-rugby-writer-athlete-childrens-author-electrician |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=The Guardian}} ==Career== === Hull Kingston Rovers === Griffin signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a two-year deal in September 2011.{{Cite web |date=2011-09-13 |title=Hull KR sign back-rower George Griffin |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/9589732.stm |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} He had been playing in Australia for the Queanbeyan Kangaroos, a feeder team to the [[Canberra Raiders]], and was originally set to sign for [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] until they withdrew from [[Super League]]. Griffin made his Super League debut for Hull KR against the [[Widnes Vikings]] on 29 July 2012.{{Cite web |date=2012-07-29 |title=Super League: Widnes 26-32 Hull Kingston Rovers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18974218 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} He went on to make a total of 20 appearances throughout his 2 seasons at Hull KR. ==== Gateshead Thunder (dual registration) ==== In 2013, Griffin played for [[Gateshead Thunder (2000)|Gateshead Thunder]] in the [[2013 Championship 1|Championship 1]] through their dual registration agreement with Hull KR. He made 3 appearances and scored 2 tries for the Thunder. === London Broncos === In January 2014, it was announced that Griffin had signed for the [[London Broncos]].{{Cite web |date=2014-01-13 |title=George Griffin: London Broncos sign Hull KR man as 12 retained |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/25712921 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} He made 20 appearances and scored 1 try in the [[Super League XIX|2014 season]]. === Salford Red Devils === In July 2014, shortly after London's relegation from Super League was mathematically confirmed, Griffin signed a one-year deal to join the [[Salford Red Devils]] for 2015.{{Cite web |date=2014-07-17 |title=Salford Red Devils: George Griffin joins from London Broncos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28349174 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} He would be joining his older brothers Darrell and Josh who already played for the club.{{Cite web |date=2014-07-18 |title=Super League: Josh Griffin pens Salford deal, Red Devils snap up brother George |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9385310/super-league-josh-griffin-pens-salford-deal-red-devils-snap-up-brother-george |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=Sky Sports}} Griffin played alongside both of his brothers for the first time at the 2015 [[Magic Weekend]] against the [[Widnes Vikings]], just days after the death of their father.{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=2015-05-29 |title=Griffin brothers to play on despite father's death |url=https://www.totalrl.com/griffin-brothers-to-play-on-despite-fathers-death/ |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=TotalRL}} He signed a two-year contract extension with Salford in June 2015.{{Cite web |date=2015-06-26 |title=George Griffin signs Salford contract extension |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/9895927/george-griffin-signs-salford-contract-extension |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=Sky Sports}} In May 2017, Griffin agreed a new two-year contract with Salford, keeping him at the club until the end of 2019.{{Cite web |date=2017-05-19 |title=George Griffin: Salford Red Devils back row agrees new contract |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/39983582 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019 |last1=Unwin |first1=Will }}{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}} Griffin made 115 appearances and scored 20 tries during his time at Salford. ==== Wakefield Trinity Wildcats (loan) ==== Griffin joined the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on a one-month loan deal in March 2015, making 5 appearances.{{Cite web |date=2015-03-18 |title=Wakefield sign George Griffin on loan from Salford and Jordan Hand from St Helens |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9764805/wakefield-sign-george-griffin-on-loan-from-salford |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=Sky Sports}} === Castleford Tigers === In July 2019, Griffin signed a two-year deal to join the [[Castleford Tigers]] from 2020.{{Cite web |date=2019-07-01 |title=George Griffin: Castleford Tigers sign Salford Red Devils forward from 2020 season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48833820 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} Head coach [[Daryl Powell]] described him as a "tough hard-working player" with a "first-class work rate and attitude".{{Cite web |date=2019-07-02 |title=Tigers confirm Griffin signing |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6443 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201107230121/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6443 |archive-date=2020-11-07 |access-date=2019-07-02 |website=Castleford Tigers |language=en}} Griffin was assigned squad number 16 for 2020.{{Cite web |date=2020-01-17 |title=2020 squad numbers revealed |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6748 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=Castleford Tigers}} He made his Castleford debut on 2 February against the [[Toronto Wolfpack]].{{Cite web |last=Hafez |first=Shamoon |date=2020-02-02 |title=Super League: Toronto Wolfpack 10-28 Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51266825 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} He scored his first try for the club against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] in the 6th round of the [[Challenge Cup]].{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=2020-09-13 |title=Challenge Cup: Castleford Tigers 16-29 Hull FC - Hull book Wigan quarter-final |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54073584 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=BBC Sport}} In May 2021, Griffin extended his contract with the Tigers for a further year.{{Cite web |date=2021-05-26 |title=George Griffin: Castleford Tigers trigger forward's contract extension until 2022 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/12317717/george-griffin-castleford-tigers-trigger-forwards-contract-extension-until-2022 |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=Sky Sports}} On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|work=BBC Sport }} Griffin was rewarded for his impressive performances in the [[2022 Castleford Tigers season|2022 season]] with a three-year contract extension in July.{{Cite web |date=28 July 2022 |title=George Griffin: Castleford forward agrees new three-year contract |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62340352 |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=28 July 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers forward George Griffin signs new contract |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-forward-george-griffin-signs-new-contract-3785984 |access-date=11 October 2025 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}} He made 26 appearances and scored 4 tries as the Tigers finished 7th, narrowly missing out on the play-offs. At the club's end-of-season awards, Griffin was named runner-up for the Player of the Year award.{{Cite tweet |number=1563265197522817027 |user=CTRLFC |title=The @GroundworkHh Player of the Season Runner Up award go to George Griffin |author=Castleford Tigers |author-link=Castleford Tigers |date=26 August 2022 |access-date=11 October 2025}} Griffin played 21 games for Castleford in the [[2023 Super League season]] as the club finished 11th on the table narrowly avoiding relegation.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/|title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed'|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=22 September 2023 }} On 28 June 2025, Griffin made his 100th appearance for Castleford in round 16 against the [[Wigan Warriors]].{{Cite tweet |number=1939036817627693217 |user=CTRLFC |title=Congratulations to George Griffin, who will be making his 100th Castleford Tigers appearance this evening! |author=Castleford Tigers |author-link=Castleford Tigers |date=28 June 2025 |access-date=11 October 2025}} In September, Castleford's Director of Rugby [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]] confirmed that Griffin would depart the club upon the expiry of his contract at the end of the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]].{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=5 September 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers coach confirms decision on off-contract stalwart’s future following Wakefield win |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coach-confirms-decision-on-off-contract-stalwarts-future-following-wakefield-win |access-date=28 September 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}} ===Sheffield Eagles=== On 16 October 2025, Griffin signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]] from 2026 on a 2-year deal.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Released Castleford Tigers stalwart lands Championship deal ahead of 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/george-griffin-lands-championship-deal-ahead-of-2026}} ==Club statistics== {{Table alignment}} {|class="wikitable defaultcenter" |+ Appearances and points in all competitions by year |- !
